metadata.description: Test-site remake of the 78th Street Studios home page using existing site content and the current strict component set.

components.headline: Northeast Ohio's eclectic arts maze
components.subheadline: A test-site remake of 78th Street Studios built from the live home and about pages: galleries, studios, events, tours, and venue rental in a 170,000-square-foot Cleveland complex.

components.title: What stands out on the live site

items.title: Scale
items.body: The site positions 78th Street Studios as the largest art and design complex in Northeast Ohio, with galleries, artist studios, performance spaces, and recording studios.

items.title: Third Fridays
items.body: Every third Friday from 5 to 9 pm, more than 60 venues open together for exhibits, music, cuisine, and pop-up vendors.

items.title: Events
items.body: The home page also pushes private events and wedding venue rentals, showing the building as both arts destination and year-round venue.

components.title: Visitor paths captured for the demo

items.title: About
items.body: History, vision, and why the building matters in Cleveland's arts scene.

items.title: Art Tours
items.body: Public and private tours are a clear next step in the main navigation.

items.title: Maps
items.body: Wayfinding matters because the building holds multiple floors of galleries and studios.

items.title: Rent Space
items.body: Venue rental is prominent on both the home page and the about copy.

components.headline: Plan a visit to 1300 West 78th Street in Cleveland
components.body: The live site highlights free public viewing hours, a contact email, and a map for first-time visitors.

metadata.description: Test-site remake of the 78th Street Studios about page using content pulled from the live site.

components.headline: A 170,000-square-foot arts and design complex
components.subheadline: The live about page describes 78th Street Studios as a Cleveland hub for art galleries, artist studios, performance spaces, music recording, product design, publishing, and special events.

components.title: Sections translated from the live about page

items.title: What it is
items.body: The building combines fine art with design, music, publishing, and special events, and the public can visit during listed open hours.

items.title: Third Fridays
items.body: The monthly Third Fridays program brings more than 60 venues together for exhibits, ambient music, cuisine, and vendors.

items.title: Growth
items.body: The page tracks the event from a small quarterly gathering in the early 2000s to a district-wide draw connected to Gordon Square.

items.title: Building history
items.body: The site traces the property from the 1905 Baker Electric Motor Vehicle Company through the American Greetings era.

items.title: Cultural footnotes
items.body: The building has hosted catalog shoots, recording projects, fan events, and tenants tied to Cleveland media and design.

components.title: Planning facts kept in the sample

items.question: When does Third Fridays happen?
items.answer: Every third Friday of the month from 5 to 9 pm.

items.question: Is the public invited?
items.answer: Yes. The about page says the listed viewing hours are free and open to the public.

items.question: Where is it?
items.answer: The footer lists 1300 West 78th Street, Cleveland, Ohio 44102.

components.headline: This demo keeps the message, not the old Drupal structure
components.body: It captures the live site's main story in the current component set, while leaving out image-heavy sections and the long tenant directory.
